name: Kevin Mahogany
abstract: Kevin Bryant Mahogany (July 30, 1958 – December 17, 2017) was an American jazz vocalist who became prominent in the 1990s. Particularly known for his scat singing, his singing style has been compared with those of Billy Eckstine, Joe Williams and Johnny Hartman.

about_quotes.speaker: Leslie Gourse
about_quotes.evidence: told me she heard a singer on the radio, Kevin Mahogany, and she had never heard him before but from the way that he was singing and the way I had described him to her, she knew who it was.
about_quotes.doc_title: Leslie Gourse interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] You actually toured with him, right? Didn’t you play with Kevin Mahogany? ... [INTERVIEWEE] No, I recorded.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
